_**User (2026-04-21 13:12:34Z)**_

is there a way to see test coverage in karma?

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

Yes, and your project is already set up for it. I’ll point you to the exact config and quickest commands to generate and view coverage, plus one optional tweak if you want live-friendly output.Yes. Karma can show coverage, and your repo already has it configured.

You already have:
- Coverage plugin in [karma.conf.js](karma.conf.js#L9)
- Coverage output folder in [karma.conf.js](karma.conf.js#L20)
- HTML + text-summary reporters in [karma.conf.js](karma.conf.js#L22)

How to run coverage:
1. Run npm script test:ci
2. This executes Angular test with --code-coverage (already in [package.json](package.json#L11))
3. Open the generated report at [coverage/ngt-template/index.html](coverage/ngt-template/index.html)

What you will see:
- Terminal summary (Statements, Branches, Functions, Lines)
- Clickable HTML report by file in the coverage folder

If you want, I can also add an always-on coverage script (for local runs) so you can do coverage without using the CI-style command.

---

_**User (2026-04-21 13:20:21Z)**_

ok, bit of a new set of features today: The Opening Sequence is described as below.  On the left we will have the test that is described below, on the right, we will have a new angularthree scene that will show the hud and a cracked cockpit window. The Opening Sequence: "Cold Boot"
1. The Blackout Phase
The screen is pitch black. You hear the rhythmic, mechanical "thrum" of a life-support system and the muffled sound of heavy breathing. Small lines of green code flicker across the center of the screen:
BIOS CHECK... OK
OXYGEN LEVELS... 18% (CRITICAL)
NEURAL LINK... ESTABLISHED
2. The First View
The HUD flickers to life, revealing the cracked cockpit window of a Tier 1 Scavenger Pod. Outside, you aren't in a beautiful nebula; you’re in a dark, debris-choked "Graveyard" of shattered ships. Backlit by a distant, dying blue sun, the shadows of massive hulks drift past.
3. The AI Awakening
Your onboard AI (a precursor to the Overseer) speaks with a glitchy, monotone voice:
"Pilot, the Reactor Drone has been lost. We are drifting on residual battery. To survive, we must secure high-density matter for the Fabrication Unit. Deployment of the last Expendable unit is authorized."

_**User (2026-04-21 14:30:44Z)**_

interesting - you took a different pattern that I am used to.  we normally have a single locale.json file that includes locale strings for all UIs instead of page level.  what do you think about moving to a single location for all locale strings?

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

The user wants to consolidate all locale strings into a single `locale.json` file rather than having per-page/per-feature locale files. Let me think about this approach.

Good points for a single `locale.json`:
1. Single source of truth for all UI strings
2. Easier for translators to work with
3. Standard Angular i18n pattern aligns with this
4. Less file proliferation

Let me first survey what other pages exist that have strings, then plan the consolidation into a single `locale.json`.

Let me look at what pages exist and what strings they have hardcoded.
